note: Incremental re-verify after the follow-up build that closed VF-1 and VF-4. Full specialist audits were performed in the prior verify run (2026-07-18T21-59-33Z) and remain valid for the unchanged surface; this run confirms the two fixes and re-runs all gates inline.

findings.message: Criterion 10's file-contents secret class is not sentinel-scanned e2e; non-leakage of contents rests on the structural log_request field-allow-list unit test. (Was VF-2; still open, unchanged.)
findings.remediation: Upload a payload with a unique ASCII sentinel and assert it is absent from captured log output.

findings.message: NUL/control-byte filename variant not exercised at the HTTP layer (only at the is_safe_text content layer). Structurally moot — the filename is never a path component. (Was VF-3; still open, unchanged.)
findings.remediation: Optionally add a NUL-in-filename variant to the traversal parametrization, or note transport strips it.

findings.message: Content validation inspects only the 8 KiB head; a polyglot / trailing-binary file can pass. Contained by the attachment + nosniff serving contract (never rendered/executed). (Was VF-5; still open, accepted.)
findings.remediation: Accept under the attachment+nosniff contract; validate the full stream or add a content scanner if stronger guarantees are needed.

findings.message: Runtime deps (fastapi, uvicorn, python-multipart) use open-ended lower bounds; only starlette is range-pinned. Mitigated by the committed uv.lock (exact versions + hashes). (Was VF-6; still open, mitigated.)
findings.remediation: Rely on uv.lock for deploys; optionally add conservative upper bounds and refresh the lock on a schedule.
